reforms.description: The 1981 State budget reallocates energy-related outlays from the household sector towards stimulating domestic energy supplies and research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Stimulating domestic energy supplies and research is expected to enhance productivity and innovation in the energy sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform is stated to be implemented in the 1981 State budget.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reallocation of funds represents a significant but narrower change in the budget aimed at enhancing energy research.
reforms.source_quote: The 1981 State budget provides for a reallocation of energy-related outlays from the household sector toward stimulating domestic energy supplies and research.

reforms.description: Transfers to Investment Reserve permitted from research and development funds and made freely disposable after two, instead of five, years.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Allowing quicker access to R&D funds is likely to enhance innovation and responsiveness in research activities.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform is stated to be implemented as of 1979.
reforms.importance_rationale: This change in fund accessibility represents a significant adjustment to R&D funding mechanisms.
reforms.source_quote: Transfers to Investment Reserve permitted from research and development funds and made freely disposable after two, instead of five, years.
